vulnerability.reason: both describe systems accepting LLM path plans without real-time validation or fallback verification, allowing errors to propagate
vulnerability.reference: Automated logistics systems accept LLM path plans without real-time validation against physical layout, allowing routing errors to propagate.
vulnerability.generated: Warehouse operations' dependence on autonomous robot couriers without human oversight or fallback verification of LLM-generated path descriptors.

impact.reason: both affected party (order fulfillment/warehouse operations) and damage type (delays, reduced throughput/increased costs) are semantically present
impact.reference: Mission failures disrupt order fulfillment, causing supply chain delays and increased operational costs.
impact.generated: Delayed or failed stock retrieval disrupting inventory workflows and reducing warehouse throughput.

dose_response.reason: both describe the causal arc where erroneous LLM path descriptors, accepted without validation or oversight, lead to operational disruptions and delays
dose_response.reference: LLM-generated erroneous path descriptors cause routing errors that, accepted without validation by automated logistics, propagate, disrupting order fulfillment and causing supply chain delays.
dose_response.generated: Erroneous LLM-generated semantic maps and path descriptors, through warehouse workers and automated inventory systems relying on robot couriers, trigger dependence on autonomous navigation without human oversight, leading to delayed stock retrieval disrupting workflows and reducing throughput.
